summary: Operational workflows are derived from the cached catalogue and safe observations.

Capability `arbitrary_execution` has not been established from the declared catalogue.

workflows.name: Read a discovered workspace item
workflows.supported_user_intent: Inspect structures reachable through safe, observed identifier traversal.
preconditions: Use only identifiers returned by a prior successful target MCP response.
Keep list or sample parameters within the profile's configured limit.

ordered_tool_sequence.purpose: Discover accessible workspace structures before selecting a collection.
Sample one observed collection with the minimum useful limit.
Read one observed item to verify the detail shape and collection linkage.

workflows.expected_result: A bounded, redacted result based on 3 successful observation(s).
optional_traversal: Traverse a relationship only when its identifier has been observed and provenance is retained.
workflows.mutation_boundary: No mutation tool is executed during onboarding.
verification_steps: Check the returned stable identifier and response shape.
failure_handling: Record the failed probe as an uncertainty and continue with other allowed probes.

evidence.claim: The sequence is supported by successful safe probe records.

workflows.name: Plan `create_item` with explicit confirmation
workflows.supported_user_intent: Prepare, but do not automatically execute, the `create_item` operation.
preconditions: Read the current state first when a safe read tool exists.

ordered_tool_sequence.purpose: Execute only after the user has reviewed exact arguments and confirmed.
ordered_tool_sequence.identifier_source: Use an observed identifier or explicit user-provided value; never invent one.

workflows.expected_result: The target-specific state change or side effect documented by the tool.
workflows.mutation_boundary: This operation was not executed during profiling.
confirmation_requirements: Present exact arguments and require explicit user confirmation immediately before execution.
verification_steps: Use a safe read tool after execution when one can verify the intended result.
failure_handling: Do not retry irreversible actions without renewed confirmation.

evidence.claim: `create_item` is declared by the target and classified by runtime policy.

workflows.name: Plan `delete_item` with explicit confirmation
workflows.supported_user_intent: Prepare, but do not automatically execute, the `delete_item` operation.
preconditions: Read the current state first when a safe read tool exists.

ordered_tool_sequence.purpose: Execute only after the user has reviewed exact arguments and confirmed.
ordered_tool_sequence.identifier_source: Use an observed identifier or explicit user-provided value; never invent one.

workflows.expected_result: The target-specific state change or side effect documented by the tool.
workflows.mutation_boundary: This operation was not executed during profiling.
confirmation_requirements: Present exact arguments and require explicit user confirmation immediately before execution.
verification_steps: Use a safe read tool after execution when one can verify the intended result.
failure_handling: Do not retry irreversible actions without renewed confirmation.

evidence.claim: `delete_item` is declared by the target and classified by runtime policy.

confirmation_boundaries: `create_item` is classified as `mutation` and requires an explicit user confirmation outside onboarding.
`delete_item` is classified as `destructive` and requires an explicit user confirmation outside onboarding.
